opening_intent: Open by telling students why the topic matters in real robot selection work.
closing_intent: Close by turning the topic into a short checklist students can use later.
lesson_arc: Start with the practical decision students need to make.
Define the technical vocabulary needed to read a datasheet.
Compare robot families by what each is best at.
End with a quick selection practice and memory check.

slides.speaking_goal: Frame the topic as a practical robot-selection skill.
slides.bridge_in: Start the class by naming the two halves of the lesson.
slides.bridge_out: Move from the overview into why specifications matter.
slides.student_memory: Students should know this lesson is about choosing robots from specs and workspace needs.
